# UC-19.1.38 · Proxmox QEMU/KVM Live Migration Task Failure Detection

## Description

Failed `qmigrate` tasks leave operators unsure whether a guest actually moved before maintenance. Tracking migration task outcomes catches stuck transfers, storage mismatches, and CPU flag incompatibilities while the maintenance window is still open.

## Value

Reduces unplanned downtime during Proxmox rolling maintenance by proving each live migration completed successfully.

## Implementation

Ingest Proxmox cluster task history (TA **Node Task** or `/var/log/pve/tasks/`). Map `qmigrate` UPIDs to `vmid` and source/target nodes. Treat non-success end states as incidents. Correlate with Corosync latency and storage latency on the path.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:task" task_type="qmigrate" earliest=-24h
| where status="ERROR" OR status="error" OR like(lower(_raw), "%error%")
| stats count by vmid, source_node, target_node, upid
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (failed migrations), Timeline (migration task stream), Single value (error count).

## References

- [Proxmox VE Wiki — QEMU/KVM Migration](https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Migration)
